title: Password Hashing, Verification, and Upgrade · Stakeholder Go Mission
summary: A regional public library identity team. They need versioned Argon2id password records, strict parser admission, enumeration-resistant verification, calibrated resource limits, pepper custody, legacy migration, successful-login upgrades, accessible recovery status, and abuse evidence. Planned artifact: Deliver Credential Verifier and Upgrade Service with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, ch…
objectives: Store password verifiers with a slow password-hashing construction rather than plaintext, reversible encryption, or fast general hashes.
Select and encode Argon2id memory, time, parallelism, salt, and output parameters from current guidance and measured service budgets.
Parse an allow-listed versioned verifier record with strict lengths, integer bounds, base64 rules, and resource admission before deriving.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Commit to the mission model · Project Crypto Go Password Hash Not Encrypt
steps.instruction:
project case — A project cryptographic engineering team handles case 1421 during a signed release review. Build deterministic Go evidence to admit a versioned password verifier, bound KDF work, and select verify-then-rehash migration; cap bytes, work, memory, time, attempts, concurrency, and retained secret material before use; then change one authenticated byte and prove clean rejection without partial effect. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?
